title: Add missing return after utils.InternalServerError()
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body:
I edited this comment and removed the discussion about including another patch. 

(It is easier to just review what is already included in this PR. The other patch was not so important anyway)
I believe you need the [NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] added, which I added, but you will need to repush.

First time the test failed rather quickly. Then I added to
```
 [NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] 
```
to the commit message and repushed. This time the tests are running without failing (it seems)

Let's see if I need to repush again.

Well ideally we would add a test for this and not `[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]`, unless there is actually a reason why this cannot be tested. I know it makes things harder for contributors but without regression tests we often break again in the future.

But more importantly we should really look into writing a custom linter/check for this, we are clearly unable to spot this in code review.

I searched around and found this file

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/apiv2/40-pods.at

Is that the file where to add a test?

Regarding  _custom linter/check_, maybe moving around `return` would make it easier to use a regexp:

Just an example 
``` diff
                 if errors.Is(err, storage.ErrImageUnknown) {
                         utils.ImageNotFound(w, name, fmt.Errorf("failed to find image %s: %w", name, err))
+                        return
                 } else {
                         utils.Error(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err)
+                        return
                 }
-                return
+
 		
```
Such an edit makes the code less beautiful but maybe it's worth the cost.

> Is that the file where to add a test?

Yes, that would be good place to add it.

I added a test. 
(I'm not sure what `.cause=` is used for so I just reused the `.message=`)
The remote debian-12 root host boltdb test timedout, I restarted.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *eriksjolund*, *rhatdan*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
